text.title: Supabase MVP Stack Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the Supabase MVP Stack methodology. Every rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale, plus the universal skip-this-methodology gate.

rule.statement: Every table MUST have RLS enabled at creation; tables without RLS are rejected on review and flagged on the audit SQL.
rule.rationale: Default-off RLS is the most common Supabase leak; default-on policy + audit query block the most severe class of leaks.

rule.statement: Policies MUST scope SELECT / INSERT / UPDATE / DELETE to `auth.uid()`, role, or explicit subqueries; `TO public USING (true)` is rejected.
rule.rationale: Permissive policies create public-data leaks; explicit scoping is the safe baseline.

rule.statement: Schema changes MUST go through `supabase migration new` and live in `supabase/migrations/`; manual dashboard SQL is rejected.
rule.rationale: Manual schema changes orphan dev environments and produce irreproducible bugs; repo migrations are the only auditable path.

rule.statement: Storage buckets MUST be private by default; public buckets require an explicit justification + bucket policy + signed-URL pattern for public reads.
rule.rationale: Public-by-default storage is the second-most-common Supabase leak; explicit privacy gates prevent.

rule.statement: Realtime subscriptions MUST go through a per-channel payload review: payloads include only columns safe for the subscriber; PII columns are rejected from public channels.
rule.rationale: Realtime over-broadcast is a quiet PII-leak vector; explicit per-channel review is the only barrier.

rule.statement: Apply this methodology only if every entry in `## Applies If` holds and none of `## Skip If` triggers; otherwise abort and route to the correct methodology.

rationale.source: F-066 spec - decision tree minimum form

rule.rationale: Misrouted methodology produces wrong-shape output; explicit skip rule is the universal default branch.
